overview: The marine iguana (Amblyrhynchus cristatus) is the only sea-dwelling lizard on Earth, found exclusively on the Galápagos Islands. They forage for algae in the ocean and use specialized glands to "sneeze" out excess salt, often leaving a white crust on their heads. To conserve energy during food shortages, such as El Niño events, they have the unique ability to shrink their body length by up to 20%. While swimming, they hold their hind limbs against their body and use their long, flattened tails for propulsion. Instagram +5 6 sites Ocean Iguanas! Sea-Going Lizards! Reptiles! in English for ...

Tomas de Berlanga (Santo Tomas), Ecuador - From your device
The marine iguana (Amblyrhynchus cristatus) is the only sea-dwelling lizard on Earth, found exclusively on the Galápagos Islands.
They forage for algae in the ocean and use specialized glands to "sneeze" out excess salt, often leaving a white crust on their heads.
To conserve energy during food shortages, such as El Niño events, they have the unique ability to shrink their body length by up to 20%.
While swimming, they hold their hind limbs against their body and use their long, flattened tails for propulsion.
